src/app/Services/files/files.service.ts
```
1 | import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
2 | import { HttpClient, HttpResponse } from '@angular/common/http';
3 | import { HttpHeaders } from '@angular/common/http';
4 | import { Observable } from 'rxjs';
5 | import { environment } from '../../../environments/environment';
6 | 
7 | @Injectable({
8 |   providedIn: 'root',
9 | })
10 | export class FileService {
11 |   private readonly apiUrl = environment.server + '/files';
12 | 
13 |   constructor(private readonly httpClient: HttpClient) {}
14 | 
15 |   /**
16 |    * Uploads a given file to the server.
17 |    *
18 |    * @param file The file to be uploaded.
19 |    * @returns An Observable containing the server response.
20 |    */
21 |   uploadFile(file: File): Observable<any> {
22 |     const formData = new FormData();
23 |     formData.append('file', file, file.name);
24 | 
25 |     return this.httpClient.post(`${this.apiUrl}/upload`, formData);
26 |   }
27 | 
28 |   /**
29 |    * Downloads a file based on a given unique identifier.
30 |    *
31 |    * @param uniqueIdentifier The unique identifier for the file to be downloaded.
32 |    * @returns An Observable containing the server response, which includes the file as a Blob.
33 |    */
34 |   downloadFile(uniqueIdentifier: string): Observable<HttpResponse<Blob>> {
35 |     return this.httpClient.get(`${this.apiUrl}/download/${uniqueIdentifier}`, {
36 |       responseType: 'blob',
37 |       observe: 'response',
38 |     });
39 |   }
40 | 
41 |   /**
42 |    * Downloads a file based on a name (first file with that name <- not unique)
43 |    *
44 |    * @param name The unique identifier for the file to be downloaded.
45 |    * @returns An Observable containing the server response, which includes the file as a Blob.
46 |    */
47 |   downloadFileByName(name: string): Observable<HttpResponse<Blob>> {
48 |     return this.httpClient.get(`${this.apiUrl}/download/byName/${name}`, {
49 |       responseType: 'blob',
50 |       observe: 'response',
51 |     });
52 |   }
53 | 
54 |   /**
55 |    * Retrieves the metadata for a file based on its unique identifier.
56 |    *
57 |    * @param uniqueIdentifier The unique identifier for the file.
58 |    * @returns An Observable containing the file metadata.
59 |    */
60 |   getFile(uniqueIdentifier: string): Observable<any> {
61 |     return this.httpClient.get(`${this.apiUrl}/${uniqueIdentifier}`);
62 |   }
63 | }
```

src/app/Services/fill-in-task/fill-in-task.service.ts
```
1 | import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
2 | import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http';
3 | import { Observable } from 'rxjs';
4 | import { BlankDTO, FillinQuestionDTO } from '@DTOs/fillInText.dto';
5 | import { environment } from 'src/environments/environment';
6 | 
7 | @Injectable({
8 |   providedIn: 'root',
9 | })
10 | export class FillInTaskService {
11 |   private readonly apiUrl = environment.server; // Replace with your actual API URL
12 | 
13 |   constructor(private readonly http: HttpClient) {}
14 | 
15 |   /**
16 |    * Get all fill-in tasks
17 |    * @returns {Observable<FillinQuestionDTO[]>} All fill-in tasks
18 |    */
19 |   getAllTasks(): Observable<FillinQuestionDTO[]> {
20 |     return this.http.get<FillinQuestionDTO[]>(this.apiUrl + `/fill-in-tasks`);
21 |   }
22 | 
23 |   /**
24 |    * Create a new fill-in task
25 |    * @param {CreateFillinQuestionDTO} createTaskDto The task data
26 |    * @returns {Observable<FillinQuestionDTO>} The created task
27 |    */
28 |   createFillInTask(createTaskDto: FillinQuestionDTO): Observable<FillinQuestionDTO> {
29 |     createTaskDto.blanks = createTaskDto.blanks.map(blank => ({
30 |       ...blank,
31 |       isDistractor: blank.isDistractor || false,
32 |     }));
33 |     console.log('sending data from frontend service: ', createTaskDto);
34 |     return this.http.post<FillinQuestionDTO>(`${this.apiUrl}/fill-in-tasks/task`, createTaskDto);
35 |   }
36 | 
37 |   /**
38 |    * Update a blank in a fill-in task
39 |    * @param {{ taskId: number, position: string, word: string }} updateBlankDto The updated blank data
40 |    * @returns {Observable<any>} The updated blank
41 |    */
42 |   updateBlank(updateBlankDto: BlankDTO): Observable<BlankDTO> {
43 |     return this.http.put<BlankDTO>(
44 |       `${this.apiUrl}/fill-in-tasks/blank/${updateBlankDto.id}`,
45 |       updateBlankDto,
46 |     );
47 |   }
48 | 
49 |   /**
50 |    * Update a fill-in task
51 |    * @param {FillinQuestionDTO} updateTaskDto The updated task data
52 |    * @returns {Observable<FillinQuestionDTO>} The updated task
53 |    */
54 |   updateFillInTask(updateTaskDto: FillinQuestionDTO): Observable<FillinQuestionDTO> {
55 |     return this.http.put<FillinQuestionDTO>(
56 |       `${this.apiUrl}/fill-in-tasks/${updateTaskDto.id}`,
57 |       updateTaskDto,
58 |     );
59 |   }
60 | 
61 |   /**
62 |    * Get a fill-in task by ID
63 |    * @param {number} id The ID of the task to get
64 |    * @returns {Observable<FillinQuestionDTO>} The task
65 |    */
66 |   getTask(id: number): Observable<FillinQuestionDTO> {
67 |     return this.http.get<FillinQuestionDTO>(`${this.apiUrl}/fill-in-tasks/task/${id}`);
68 |   }
69 | 
70 |   /**
71 |    * Delete a fill-in task
72 |    * @param {number} id The ID of the task to delete
73 |    * @returns {Observable<FillinQuestionDTO>} The deleted task
74 |    */
75 |   deleteTask(id: number): Observable<FillinQuestionDTO> {
76 |     return this.http.delete<FillinQuestionDTO>(`${this.apiUrl}/${id}`);
77 |   }
78 | }
```

src/app/Services/graph/graph-data.service.ts
```
1 | import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http';
2 | import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
3 | 
4 | import { ConceptGraphDTO } from '@DTOs/index';
5 | import { Observable } from 'rxjs';
6 | import { environment } from 'src/environments/environment';
7 | 
8 | @Injectable({
9 |   providedIn: 'root',
10 | })
11 | export class GraphDataService {
12 |   constructor(private readonly http: HttpClient) {}
13 | 
14 |   /**
15 |    * Fetches the graph for a specific user
16 |    * with the user's progress
17 |    * @param moduleId The id of the module (for the concept goals)
18 |    * @returns The graph for the user
19 |    */
20 |   fetchUserGraph(moduleId: number): Observable<ConceptGraphDTO> {
21 |     return this.http.get<ConceptGraphDTO>(environment.server + `/graph/${moduleId}`);
22 |   }
23 | 
24 |   /**
25 |    * Creates a new concept node and returns it
26 |    * @param parentId The id of the parent concept
27 |    * @param conceptName The name of the new concept
28 |    * @returns The new concept node
29 |    */
30 |   createConcept(
31 |     parentId: string,
32 |     conceptName: string,
33 |     description?: string,
34 |     moduleGoals?: { moduleId: number; goal: number }[],
35 |   ) {
36 |     //console.log("in graph-data.service. Trying to create concept: ", parentId, conceptName)
37 |     const x = this.http
38 |       .post(environment.server + `/graph/concept/${parentId}/${conceptName}`, {
39 |         description: description,
40 |         moduleGoals: moduleGoals,
41 |       })
42 |       .subscribe();
43 |     //console.log("x: ", x)
44 |     return x;
45 |   }
46 | 
47 |   /**
48 |    * Deletes a concept node if it has no children
49 |    * @param conceptId The id of the concept node
50 |    * @returns The deleted concept node
51 |    * @throws Will throw an error if the concept node has children
52 |    */
53 |   deleteConcept(conceptId: number) {
54 |     //console.log("trying to delete node with id: ", conceptId)
55 |     return this.http.delete(environment.server + `/graph/concept/${conceptId}`);
56 |   }
57 | 
58 |   /**
59 |    * Creates a new edge between two concepts, if it doesn't already exist
60 |    * @param parentId The id of the parent concept (usually the parent of the connected concepts)
61 |    * @param prerequisite The id of the prerequisite concept
62 |    * @param successor The id of the successor concept
63 |    * @returns The new edge
64 |    */
65 |   createEdge(parentId: number, prerequisiteId: number, successorId: number) {
66 |     //console.log("in createEdge: ", parentId, prerequisiteId, successorId)
67 |     return this.http.post(environment.server + `/graph/edge`, {
68 |       parentId: parentId,
69 |       prerequisiteId: prerequisiteId,
70 |       successorId: successorId,
71 |     });
72 |   }
73 | 
74 |   deleteEdge(edgeId: number) {
75 |     //console.log("in deleteEdge: ", edgeId)
76 |     return this.http.delete(environment.server + `/graph/edge/${edgeId}`);
77 |   }
78 | 
79 |   moveConceptNode(conceptId: number, parentId: number) {
80 |     //console.log("in moveConcept: ", conceptId, parentId)
81 |     return this.http.put(
82 |       environment.server + `/graph/concept/${conceptId}/newParent/${parentId}`,
83 |       {},
84 |     );
85 |   }
86 | 
87 |   updateUserLevel(conceptId: number, level: number) {
88 |     //console.log("in updateUserLevel: ", level)
89 |     return this.http.put(environment.server + `/user-concept/${conceptId}/level/${level}`, {});
90 |   }
91 | 
92 |   updateSelectedConcept(conceptId: number) {
93 |     return this.http.put(environment.server + `/user-concept/concept/${conceptId}/selected`, {});
94 |   }
95 | 
96 |   updateConceptExpansionState(conceptId: number, expanded: boolean) {
97 |     return this.http.put(
98 |       environment.server + `/user-concept/${conceptId}/expanded/${expanded}`,
99 |       {},
100 |     );
101 |   }
102 | }
```

src/app/Services/graph/graphCommunication.service.ts
```
1 | import { ConceptNodeDTO } from '@DTOs/conceptNode.dto';
2 | import { BehaviorSubject, Subject } from 'rxjs';
3 | 
4 | export class GraphCommunicationService {
5 |   private static instance: GraphCommunicationService;
6 | 
7 |   // init with dummy node
8 |   dummyNode: ConceptNodeDTO = {
9 |     databaseId: 0,
10 |     name: 'dummy',
11 |     level: 0,
12 |     expanded: false,
13 |     parentIds: [],
14 |     childIds: [],
15 |     prerequisiteEdgeIds: [],
16 |     successorEdgeIds: [],
17 |     edgeChildIds: [],
18 |   };
19 | 
20 |   private readonly ActiveNode = new BehaviorSubject<ConceptNodeDTO>(this.dummyNode);
21 |   currentActiveNode = this.ActiveNode.asObservable();
22 | 
23 |   private readonly graphUpdateTrigger = new Subject<void>();
24 |   graphUpdateNeeded = this.graphUpdateTrigger.asObservable();
25 | 
26 |   private constructor() {}
27 | 
28 |   // Singleton pattern
29 |   public static getInstance(): GraphCommunicationService {
30 |     if (!GraphCommunicationService.instance) {
31 |       GraphCommunicationService.instance = new GraphCommunicationService();
32 |     }
33 |     return GraphCommunicationService.instance;
34 |   }
35 | 
36 |   // communication between graph and contentOverview
37 |   changeActiveNode(ActiveNode: ConceptNodeDTO) {
38 |     this.ActiveNode.next(ActiveNode);
39 |   }
40 | 
41 |   // Trigger graph update (graph/sprotty/model.source.ts is listening)
42 |   triggerGraphUpdate() {
43 |     this.graphUpdateTrigger.next();
44 |   }
45 | }
```

src/app/Services/highlight-concepts/highlight-concepts.service.ts
```
1 | import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
2 | import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http';
3 | import { Observable } from 'rxjs';
4 | import { environment } from '../../../environments/environment';
5 | import { UserService } from '../auth/user.service';
6 | import {
7 |   HighlightConceptDto,
8 |   CreateHighlightConceptDto,
9 |   UpdateHighlightConceptDto,
10 | } from '@DTOs/index';
11 | 
12 | @Injectable({
13 |   providedIn: 'root',
14 | })
15 | export class HighlightConceptsService {
16 |   private readonly apiUrl = `${environment.server}/highlight-concepts`;
17 | 
18 |   constructor(
19 |     private readonly http: HttpClient,
20 |     private readonly userService: UserService,
21 |   ) {}
22 | 
23 |   /**
24 |    * Gets all highlight concepts for a specific module
25 |    * @param moduleId The ID of the module
26 |    * @returns An Observable of HighlightConcept array
27 |    */
28 |   getHighlightConcepts(moduleId: number): Observable<HighlightConceptDto[]> {
29 |     return this.http.get<HighlightConceptDto[]>(`${this.apiUrl}/module/${moduleId}`);
30 |   }
31 | 
32 |   /**
33 |    * Gets a specific highlight concept by ID
34 |    * @param id The ID of the highlight concept
35 |    * @returns An Observable of HighlightConcept
36 |    */
37 |   getHighlightConcept(id: number): Observable<HighlightConceptDto> {
38 |     return this.http.get<HighlightConceptDto>(`${this.apiUrl}/${id}`);
39 |   }
40 | 
41 |   /**
42 |    * Creates a new highlight concept
43 |    * Only available for ADMIN users
44 |    * @param data The data for the new highlight concept
45 |    * @returns An Observable of the created HighlightConcept
46 |    */
47 |   createHighlightConcept(data: CreateHighlightConceptDto): Observable<HighlightConceptDto> {
48 |     return this.http.post<HighlightConceptDto>(this.apiUrl, data);
49 |   }
50 | 
51 |   /**
52 |    * Updates an existing highlight concept
53 |    * Only available for ADMIN users
54 |    * @param id The ID of the highlight concept to update
55 |    * @param data The data to update
56 |    * @returns An Observable of the updated HighlightConcept
57 |    */
58 |   updateHighlightConcept(
59 |     id: number,
60 |     data: UpdateHighlightConceptDto,
61 |   ): Observable<HighlightConceptDto> {
62 |     return this.http.put<HighlightConceptDto>(`${this.apiUrl}/${id}`, data);
63 |   }
64 | 
65 |   /**
66 |    * Deletes a highlight concept
67 |    * Only available for ADMIN users
68 |    * @param id The ID of the highlight concept to delete
69 |    * @returns An Observable of the deletion result
70 |    */
71 |   deleteHighlightConcept(id: number): Observable<any> {
72 |     return this.http.delete<any>(`${this.apiUrl}/${id}`);
73 |   }
74 | }
```

src/app/Services/mcqCreation/mcqcreation.service.ts
```
1 | import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
2 | import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http';
3 | import { HttpParams } from '@angular/common/http';
4 | import { Observable } from 'rxjs/internal/Observable';
5 | import { environment } from 'src/environments/environment';
6 | import { McqGenerationDTO } from '@DTOs/question.dto';
7 | import { OptionDTO } from '@DTOs/question.dto';
8 | import { Answer, McqEvaluations } from './mcqcreation.types';
9 | import { McqEvaluation } from './mcqcreation.types';
10 | 
11 | @Injectable({
12 |   providedIn: 'root',
13 | })
14 | export class McqcreationService {
15 |   constructor(private readonly http: HttpClient) {}
16 | 
17 |   /**
18 |    *
19 |    * @param question
20 |    * @param option
21 |    * @param otherOptions
22 |    * @param concept
23 |    * @returns single autogenerated answer
24 |    */
25 |   getAnswer(
26 |     question: string,
27 |     option: OptionDTO,
28 |     otherOptions: OptionDTO[],
29 |     concept: string,
30 |   ): Observable<Answer> {
31 |     const body = {
32 |       question: question,
33 |       option: option,
34 |       otherOptions: otherOptions,
35 |       concept: concept,
36 |     };
37 |     return this.http.post<Answer>(environment.server + `/mcqcreation/answer`, body);
38 |   }
39 | 
40 |   /**
41 |    * Get the autogenerated answers for a given question
42 |    * @param question The question to answer
43 |    * @param options The number of options to generate
44 |    * @returns autogenerated answers
45 |    */
46 |   getAnswers(question: string, options: number, concept: string): Observable<McqGenerationDTO> {
47 |     const body = new HttpParams()
48 |       .set('question', question)
49 |       .set('options', options)
50 |       .set('concept', concept);
51 | 
52 |     return this.http.get<McqGenerationDTO>(environment.server + `/mcqcreation/answers`, {
53 |       params: body,
54 |     });
55 |   }
56 | 
57 |   /**
58 |    *
59 |    * @param concept
60 |    * @param options
61 |    * @returns generated question and answers
62 |    */
63 |   getQuestionAndAnswers(
64 |     concept: string,
65 |     options: number,
66 |     topic: string = '',
67 |   ): Observable<McqGenerationDTO> {
68 |     const body = new HttpParams()
69 |       .set('concept', concept)
70 |       .set('options', options)
71 |       .set('topic', topic);
72 |     return this.http.get<McqGenerationDTO>(environment.server + `/mcqcreation/questionAndAnswers`, {
73 |       params: body,
74 |     });
75 |   }
76 | 
77 |   /**
78 |    *
79 |    * @param question
80 |    * @returns
81 |    */
82 |   getQuestionTitle(concept: string): Observable<{ question: string }> {
83 |     const body = new HttpParams().set('concept', concept);
84 |     return this.http.get<{ question: string }>(environment.server + `/mcqcreation/questionTitle`, {
85 |       params: body,
86 |     });
87 |   }
88 | 
89 |   /**
90 |    * Get evaluation for the answers
91 |    * @param question The question text
92 |    * @param answers Array of answer texts
93 |    * @returns Observable of evaluations with reasoning for each answer
94 |    */
95 |   getEvaluation(question: string, answers: string[]): Observable<McqEvaluations> {
96 |     const body = new HttpParams().set('question', question).set('answers', answers.join(','));
97 |     return this.http.get<McqEvaluations>(environment.server + `/mcqcreation/evaluateOptions`, {
98 |       params: body,
99 |     });
100 |   }
101 | 
102 |   /** NOT IMPLEMENTED
103 |    * @param question
104 |    * @param options
105 |    * @param concept
106 |    * @param answers
107 |    * @returns reevaluated question and answers
108 |    */
109 |   getReevaluatedQuestionAndAnswers(
110 |     question: string,
111 |     options: number,
112 |     concept: string,
113 |     answers: string,
114 |   ): Observable<{ mcq: McqGenerationDTO; reasoning: string }> {
115 |     const body = new HttpParams()
116 |       .set('question', question)
117 |       .set('options', options)
118 |       .set('concept', concept)
119 |       .set('answers', answers);
120 |     return this.http.get<{ mcq: McqGenerationDTO; reasoning: string }>(
121 |       environment.server + `/mcqcreation/reevaluatedQuestionAndAnswers`,
122 |       { params: body },
123 |     );
124 |   }
125 | }
```

src/app/Services/mobile/screen-size.service.ts
```
1 | import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
2 | import { BreakpointObserver } from '@angular/cdk/layout';
3 | import { BreakpointState, Breakpoints } from '@angular/cdk/layout';
4 | import { Observable } from 'rxjs';
5 | import { map } from 'rxjs/operators';
6 | 
7 | @Injectable({
8 |   providedIn: 'root',
9 | })
10 | export class ScreenSizeService {
11 |   isHandset: Observable<boolean>;
12 |   isWeb: Observable<boolean>;
13 |   isTablet: Observable<boolean>;
14 |   isLandscape: Observable<boolean>;
15 | 
16 |   constructor(private readonly breakpointObserver: BreakpointObserver) {
17 |     this.isHandset = this.breakpointObserver
18 |       .observe([Breakpoints.Handset])
19 |       .pipe(map(result => result.matches));
20 | 
21 |     this.isWeb = this.breakpointObserver
22 |       .observe([Breakpoints.Web])
23 |       .pipe(map(result => result.matches));
24 | 
25 |     this.isTablet = this.breakpointObserver
26 |       .observe([Breakpoints.Tablet])
27 |       .pipe(map(result => result.matches));
28 | 
29 |     this.isLandscape = this.breakpointObserver
30 |       .observe('(orientation: landscape)')
31 |       .pipe(map(result => result.matches));
32 |   }
33 | }

src/app/Services/module-settings/module-settings.service.ts
```
1 | import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
2 | import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http';
3 | import { Observable } from 'rxjs';
4 | import { map } from 'rxjs';
5 | import { environment } from '../../../environments/environment';
6 | 
7 | @Injectable({
8 |   providedIn: 'root',
9 | })
10 | export class ModuleSettingsService {
11 |   private readonly apiUrl = environment.server;
12 | 
13 |   constructor(private readonly http: HttpClient) {}
14 | 
15 |   /**
16 |    * Gets the settings for a specific module and key
17 |    */
18 |   getSetting<T>(moduleId: number, key: string): Observable<T> {
19 |     console.log('moduleId in getsetting', moduleId);
20 |     console.log('key in getsetting', key);
21 |     return this.http
22 |       .get<{ value: string }>(`${this.apiUrl}/module-settings/${moduleId}/${key}`)
23 |       .pipe(map(response => JSON.parse(response.value) as T));
24 |   }
25 | 
26 |   /**
27 |    * Updates settings for a specific module and key
28 |    */
29 |   updateSetting<T>(moduleId: number, key: string, value: T): Observable<void> {
30 |     return this.http.post<void>(`${this.apiUrl}/module-settings/${moduleId}/${key}`, {
31 |       value: JSON.stringify(value),
32 |     });
33 |   }
34 | }
```

src/app/Services/navigation/navigation-preference.service.ts
```
1 | import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
2 | import { Observable } from 'rxjs';
3 | import { BehaviorSubject, of } from 'rxjs';
4 | import { ModuleSettingsService } from '../module-settings/module-settings.service';
5 | import { catchError, map } from 'rxjs/operators';
6 | 
7 | export type NavigationType = 'graph' | 'mobile' | 'highlight';
8 | 
9 | interface NavigatorSetting {
10 |   enabled: NavigationType[];
11 | }
12 | 
13 | @Injectable({
14 |   providedIn: 'root',
15 | })
16 | export class NavigationPreferenceService {
17 |   private readonly STORAGE_KEY = 'nav_preference';
18 |   private readonly preferenceSubject = new BehaviorSubject<NavigationType>(
19 |     this.getStoredPreference(),
20 |   );
21 |   private enabledNavigationTypes: NavigationType[] = ['graph', 'mobile', 'highlight'];
22 | 
23 |   /**
24 |    * Returns the current navigation preference as an Observable
25 |    */
26 |   get preference$(): Observable<NavigationType> {
27 |     return this.preferenceSubject.asObservable();
28 |   }
29 | 
30 |   /**
31 |    * Returns the current navigation preference value
32 |    */
33 |   get currentPreference(): NavigationType {
34 |     return this.preferenceSubject.value;
35 |   }
36 | 
37 |   /**
38 |    * Retrieves the stored navigation preference from localStorage
39 |    * Defaults to 'graph' if no preference is stored
40 |    */
41 |   private getStoredPreference(): NavigationType {
42 |     const storedPreference = localStorage.getItem(this.STORAGE_KEY) as NavigationType;
43 |     return storedPreference || 'graph';
44 |   }
45 | 
46 |   /**
47 |    * Sets and stores the user's navigation preference
48 |    * @param preference The navigation preference to set ('graph' or 'mobile')
49 |    */
50 |   setNavigationPreference(preference: NavigationType): void {
51 |     localStorage.setItem(this.STORAGE_KEY, preference);
52 |     this.preferenceSubject.next(preference);
53 |   }
54 | 
55 |   /**
56 |    * Toggles between enabled navigation types in sequence
57 |    */
58 |   toggleNavigationPreference(): void {
59 |     // Get the index of the current preference in the enabled types
60 |     const currentIndex = this.enabledNavigationTypes.indexOf(this.currentPreference);
61 | 
62 |     // If current preference is not in enabled types or it's the last one, go to the first enabled type
63 |     // Otherwise, go to the next enabled type
64 |     const nextIndex =
65 |       currentIndex === -1 || currentIndex === this.enabledNavigationTypes.length - 1
66 |         ? 0
67 |         : currentIndex + 1;
68 | 
69 |     // Set the new preference
70 |     const newPreference = this.enabledNavigationTypes[nextIndex];
71 |     this.setNavigationPreference(newPreference);
72 |   }
73 | 
74 |   /**
75 |    * Loads the enabled navigation types from the server
76 |    * @param moduleId The ID of the module
77 |    */
78 |   loadEnabledNavigationTypes(moduleId: number): Observable<NavigationType[]> {
79 |     console.log('loadEnabledNavigationTypes', moduleId);
80 |     return this.moduleSettings.getSetting<NavigatorSetting>(moduleId, 'enabled_navigators').pipe(
81 |       map(setting => {
82 |         if (setting?.enabled && setting.enabled.length > 0) {
83 |           this.enabledNavigationTypes = setting.enabled;
84 | 
85 |           // If current preference is not in enabled types, switch to the first enabled type
86 |           if (!this.enabledNavigationTypes.includes(this.currentPreference)) {
87 |             this.setNavigationPreference(this.enabledNavigationTypes[0]);
88 |           }
89 | 
90 |           return this.enabledNavigationTypes;
91 |         }
92 |         return ['graph', 'mobile', 'highlight'] as NavigationType[];
93 |       }),
94 |       catchError(() => {
95 |         // Default to all navigation types if there's an error
96 |         this.enabledNavigationTypes = ['graph', 'mobile', 'highlight'] as NavigationType[];
97 |         return of(this.enabledNavigationTypes);
98 |       }),
99 |     );
100 |   }
101 | 
102 |   constructor(private readonly moduleSettings: ModuleSettingsService) {
103 |     // Load enabled navigation types for module ID 1 (default module)
104 |     this.loadEnabledNavigationTypes(1).subscribe();
105 |   }
106 | }
```

src/app/Services/notification/notification.service.spec.ts
```
1 | import { TestBed, fakeAsync, tick } from '@angular/core/testing';
2 | import { NotificationService } from './notification.service';
3 | import { HttpTestingController, provideHttpClientTesting } from '@angular/common/http/testing';
4 | import { UserService } from '../auth/user.service';
5 | import { MatSnackBar } from '@angular/material/snack-bar';
6 | import { Router } from '@angular/router';
7 | import { WebSocketService } from '../websocket/websocket.service';
8 | import { BehaviorSubject, of } from 'rxjs';
9 | import { NotificationDTO } from '@DTOs/notification.dto';
10 | import { NotificationType } from '@DTOs/notificationType.enum';
11 | import { environment } from 'src/environments/environment';
12 | import { provideHttpClient, withInterceptorsFromDi } from '@angular/common/http';
13 | 
14 | describe('NotificationService', () => {
15 |   let service: NotificationService;
16 |   let httpMock: HttpTestingController;
17 |   let userServiceMock: jasmine.SpyObj<UserService>;
18 |   let snackBarMock: jasmine.SpyObj<MatSnackBar>;
19 |   let routerMock: jasmine.SpyObj<Router>;
20 |   let webSocketServiceMock: jasmine.SpyObj<WebSocketService>;
21 | 
22 |   beforeEach(() => {
23 |     const userServiceSpy = jasmine.createSpyObj('UserService', ['getTokenID'], {
24 |       isAuthenticated$: new BehaviorSubject<boolean>(true),
25 |     });
26 |     const snackBarSpy = jasmine.createSpyObj('MatSnackBar', ['openFromComponent']);
27 |     const routerSpy = jasmine.createSpyObj('Router', ['navigate']);
28 |     const webSocketServiceSpy = jasmine.createSpyObj('WebSocketService', [
29 |       'connect',
30 |       'disconnect',
31 |       'on',
32 |       'onConnectionChange',
33 |       'emit',
34 |     ]);
35 | 
36 |     TestBed.configureTestingModule({
37 |       imports: [],
38 |       providers: [
39 |         NotificationService,
40 |         { provide: UserService, useValue: userServiceSpy },
41 |         { provide: MatSnackBar, useValue: snackBarSpy },
42 |         { provide: Router, useValue: routerSpy },
43 |         { provide: WebSocketService, useValue: webSocketServiceSpy },
44 |         provideHttpClient(withInterceptorsFromDi()),
45 |         provideHttpClientTesting(),
46 |       ],
47 |     });
48 | 
49 |     service = TestBed.inject(NotificationService);
50 |     httpMock = TestBed.inject(HttpTestingController);
51 |     userServiceMock = TestBed.inject(UserService) as jasmine.SpyObj<UserService>;
52 |     snackBarMock = TestBed.inject(MatSnackBar) as jasmine.SpyObj<MatSnackBar>;
53 |     routerMock = TestBed.inject(Router) as jasmine.SpyObj<Router>;
54 |     webSocketServiceMock = TestBed.inject(WebSocketService) as jasmine.SpyObj<WebSocketService>;
55 | 
56 |     userServiceMock.getTokenID.and.returnValue('1');
57 |     webSocketServiceMock.onConnectionChange.and.returnValue(of(true));
58 |     webSocketServiceMock.on.and.returnValue(of({}));
59 |   });
60 | 
61 |   afterEach(() => {
62 |     httpMock.verify();
63 |   });
64 | 
65 |   it('should be created', () => {
66 |     expect(service).toBeTruthy();
67 |   });
68 | 
69 |   it('should fetch initial notifications on creation', fakeAsync(() => {
70 |     const mockNotifications: NotificationDTO[] = [
71 |       {
72 |         id: 1,
73 |         message: 'Test notification',
74 |         timestamp: new Date(),
75 |         type: NotificationType.COMMENT,
76 |         isRead: false,
77 |       },
78 |     ];
79 | 
80 |     service.getNotifications().subscribe(notifications => {
81 |       expect(notifications).toEqual(mockNotifications);
82 |     });
83 | 
84 |     const req = httpMock.expectOne(
85 |       `${environment.server}/notifications/all?userId=1&limit=20&offset=0`,
86 |     );
87 |     expect(req.request.method).toBe('GET');
88 |     req.flush(mockNotifications);
89 | 
90 |     tick();
91 |   }));
92 | 
93 |   it('should handle new notifications', fakeAsync(() => {
94 |     const mockNotification: Partial<NotificationDTO> = {
95 |       id: 1,
96 |       message: 'New notification',
97 |       timestamp: new Date(),
98 |       type: NotificationType.COMMENT,
99 |     };
100 | 
101 |     webSocketServiceMock.on.and.returnValue(of(mockNotification));
102 | 
103 |     service.initializeWebSocket();
104 |     tick();
105 | 
106 |     service.getNotifications().subscribe(notifications => {
107 |       expect(notifications[0]).toEqual(jasmine.objectContaining(mockNotification));
108 |     });
109 | 
110 |     expect(snackBarMock.openFromComponent).toHaveBeenCalled();
111 |   }));
112 | 
113 |   it('should mark notification as read', fakeAsync(() => {
114 |     const mockNotification: NotificationDTO = {
115 |       id: 1,
116 |       message: 'Test notification',
117 |       timestamp: new Date(),
118 |       type: NotificationType.COMMENT,
119 |       isRead: false,
120 |     };
121 | 
122 |     service.markNotificationAsRead(mockNotification).subscribe(updatedNotification => {
123 |       expect(updatedNotification.isRead).toBe(true);
124 |     });
125 | 
126 |     const req = httpMock.expectOne(`${environment.server}/notifications/1/read`);
127 |     expect(req.request.method).toBe('PATCH');
128 |     req.flush({ ...mockNotification, isRead: true });
129 | 
130 |     tick();
131 |   }));
132 | 
133 |   it('should handle notification click for unread notification', fakeAsync(() => {
134 |     const mockNotification: NotificationDTO = {
135 |       id: 1,
136 |       message: 'Test notification',
137 |       timestamp: new Date(),
138 |       type: NotificationType.COMMENT,
139 |       isRead: false,
140 |       discussionId: 123,
141 |     };
142 | 
143 |     service.handleNotificationClick(mockNotification, 'view').subscribe();
144 | 
145 |     const req = httpMock.expectOne(`${environment.server}/notifications/1/read`);
146 |     expect(req.request.method).toBe('PATCH');
147 |     req.flush({ ...mockNotification, isRead: true });
148 | 
149 |     tick();
150 | 
151 |     expect(routerMock.navigate).toHaveBeenCalledWith(['/discussion-view/123']);
152 |   }));
153 | 
154 |   it('should update unread count', fakeAsync(() => {
155 |     service.getUnreadCountFromServer().subscribe(count => {
156 |       expect(count).toBe(5);
157 |     });
158 | 
159 |     const req = httpMock.expectOne(`${environment.server}/notifications/1/unread-count`);
160 |     expect(req.request.method).toBe('GET');
161 |     req.flush(5);
162 | 
163 |     tick();
164 | 
165 |     service.getUnreadCount().subscribe(count => {
166 |       expect(count).toBe(5);
167 |     });
168 |   }));
169 | });
```

src/app/Services/notification/notification.service.ts
```
1 | import { OnDestroy } from '@angular/core';
2 | import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
3 | import { Subscription } from 'rxjs';
4 | import { BehaviorSubject, Observable, of } from 'rxjs';
5 | import { catchError, filter, map, switchMap } from 'rxjs/operators';
6 | import { NotificationType } from '@DTOs/notificationType.enum';
7 | import { UserService } from '../auth/user.service';
8 | import { environment } from 'src/environments/environment';
9 | import { NotificationDTO } from '@DTOs/notification.dto';
10 | import { Router } from '@angular/router';
11 | import { MatSnackBar } from '@angular/material/snack-bar';
12 | import { NotificationComponent } from 'src/app/Pages/notification/notification.component';
13 | import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http';
14 | import { WebSocketService } from '../websocket/websocket.service';
15 | 
16 | /**
17 |  * Service responsible for managing notifications in the frontend.
18 |  * Handles real-time notifications via WebSocket, fetches notifications from the server,
19 |  * and provides methods to interact with notifications.
20 |  */
21 | @Injectable({
22 |   providedIn: 'root',
23 | })
24 | export class NotificationService implements OnDestroy {
25 |   private readonly NOTIFICATION_NAMESPACE = 'notifications';
26 |   private readonly NOTIFICATION_RECEIVEEVENT = 'notification';
27 |   private readonly NOTIFICATION_SENDEVENT = 'notifications';
28 |   private readonly BROADCAST_CHANNEL_NAME = 'unreadCountChannel';
29 |   private readonly NOTIFICATION_TYPES = {
30 |     NEW_NOTIFICATION: 'newNotification',
31 |     UNREAD_COUNT: 'unreadCount',
32 |     NOTIFICATION_READ: 'notificationRead',
33 |   };
34 |   private readonly NOTIFICATION_ACTIONS = {
35 |     VIEW: 'view',
36 |   };
37 |   /** BehaviorSubject to store and emit notifications */
38 |   private readonly notificationsSubject = new BehaviorSubject<NotificationDTO[]>([]);
39 |   /** Array to store notifications */
40 |   private notifications: NotificationDTO[] = [];
41 |   /** ID of the current user */
42 |   private userId: number | undefined;
43 |   /** BehaviorSubject to store and emit the count of unread notifications */
44 |   private readonly unreadCountSubject = new BehaviorSubject<number>(0);
45 |   /** BroadcastChannel for cross-tab communication */
46 |   private readonly broadcastChannel: BroadcastChannel;
47 |   /** Set to keep track of processed notifications to avoid duplicates */
48 |   private readonly processedNotifications = new Set<string>();
49 |   /** BehaviorSubject to control closing of the notification panel */
50 |   private readonly closeNotificationPanelSubject = new BehaviorSubject<boolean>(false);
51 |   /** Observable for closing the notification panel */
52 |   closeNotificationPanel$ = this.closeNotificationPanelSubject.asObservable();
53 |   /** BehaviorSubject to trigger refreshing of discussions */
54 |   private readonly refreshDiscussionSubject = new BehaviorSubject<number | null>(null);
55 |   /** Observable for refreshing discussions */
56 |   refreshDiscussion$ = this.refreshDiscussionSubject.asObservable();
57 |   /** Subscription for WebSocket notifications */
58 |   private notificationSubscription?: Subscription;
59 | 
60 |   /**
61 |    * Creates an instance of NotificationService.
62 |    * @param {UserService} userService - Service to handle user-related operations
63 |    * @param {MatSnackBar} snackBar - Service to show snackbar notifications
64 |    * @param {Router} router - Angular router for navigation
65 |    * @param {HttpClient} http - HttpClient for making HTTP requests
66 |    * @param {WebSocketService} webSocketService - Service for WebSocket communications
67 |    */
68 |   constructor(
69 |     private readonly userService: UserService,
70 |     private readonly snackBar: MatSnackBar,
71 |     private readonly router: Router,
72 |     private readonly http: HttpClient,
73 |     private readonly webSocketService: WebSocketService,
74 |   ) {
75 |     this.broadcastChannel = new BroadcastChannel(this.BROADCAST_CHANNEL_NAME);
76 |     this.userService.isAuthenticated$.subscribe(isAuthenticated => {
77 |       if (isAuthenticated) {
78 |         this.userId = Number(this.userService.getTokenID());
79 |         this.startListening();
80 |         this.syncUnreadCount();
81 |         this.setupBroadcastChannel();
82 |       } else {
83 |         this.stopListening();
84 |       }
85 |     });
86 |   }
87 | 
88 |   /**
89 |    * Lifecycle hook that performs cleanup on service destruction.
90 |    */
91 |   ngOnDestroy() {
92 |     this.stopListening();
93 |     if (this.notificationSubscription) {
94 |       this.notificationSubscription.unsubscribe();
95 |     }
96 |   }
97 | 
98 |   initializeWebSocket(): void {
99 |     if (!this.webSocketService.isConnected(environment.websocketUrl, this.NOTIFICATION_NAMESPACE)) {
100 |       this.startListening();
101 |     }
102 |   }
103 | 
104 |   /**
105 |    * Sets up the broadcast channel to communicate with other tabs.
106 |    * Handles three types of synchronization:
107 |    * 1. New incoming notifications
108 |    * 2. Unread count of the notification bell
109 |    * 3. Notifications being read
110 |    */
111 |   private setupBroadcastChannel(): void {
112 |     this.broadcastChannel.onmessage = event => {
113 |       switch (event.data.type) {
114 |         case this.NOTIFICATION_TYPES.NEW_NOTIFICATION:
115 |           this.handleNewNotification(event.data.notification, false);
116 |           break;
117 |         case this.NOTIFICATION_TYPES.UNREAD_COUNT:
118 |           this.setUnreadCount(event.data.count, false);
119 |           break;
120 |         case this.NOTIFICATION_TYPES.NOTIFICATION_READ:
121 |           this.updateNotificationReadStatus(event.data.notificationId);
122 |           this.syncUnreadCount();
123 |           break;
124 |       }
125 |     };
126 |   }
127 | 
128 |   /**
129 |    * Updates the read status of a notification locally.
130 |    * @param {number} notificationId - ID of the notification to update
131 |    */
132 |   private updateNotificationReadStatus(notificationId: number): void {
133 |     const notification = this.notifications.find(n => n.id === notificationId);
134 |     if (notification) {
135 |       notification.isRead = true;
136 |       this.notificationsSubject.next([...this.notifications]);
137 |     }
138 |   }
139 | 
140 |   /**
141 |    * Triggers a refresh for a specific discussion.
142 |    * @param {number} discussionId - ID of the discussion to refresh
143 |    */
144 |   triggerDiscussionRefresh(discussionId: number) {
145 |     this.refreshDiscussionSubject.next(discussionId);
146 |   }
147 | 
148 |   /**
149 |    * Starts listening for notifications and establishes a connection to the WebSocket.
150 |    */
151 |   private startListening(): void {
152 |     this.fetchInitialNotifications(20, 0).subscribe();
153 |     this.webSocketService.connect(environment.websocketUrl, this.NOTIFICATION_NAMESPACE);
154 | 
155 |     this.notificationSubscription = this.webSocketService
156 |       .onConnectionChange(environment.websocketUrl, this.NOTIFICATION_NAMESPACE)
157 |       .pipe(
158 |         filter((connected: boolean) => connected),
159 |         switchMap(() =>
160 |           this.webSocketService.on<Partial<NotificationDTO>>(
161 |             environment.websocketUrl,
162 |             this.NOTIFICATION_NAMESPACE,
163 |             this.NOTIFICATION_RECEIVEEVENT,
164 |           ),
165 |         ),
166 |       )
167 |       .subscribe({
168 |         next: (notification: Partial<NotificationDTO>) => {
169 |           console.log('WebSocket connected and received notification:', notification);
170 |           this.handleNewNotification(notification, true);
171 |         },
172 |         error: error => console.error('WebSocket error:', error),
173 |         complete: () => console.log('WebSocket connection closed'),
174 |       });
175 |   }
176 | 
177 |   /**
178 |    * Stops listening for notifications and disconnects from the WebSocket.
179 |    */
180 |   private stopListening(): void {
181 |     this.webSocketService.disconnect(environment.websocketUrl, this.NOTIFICATION_NAMESPACE);
182 |   }
183 | 
184 |   /**
185 |    * Handles a new notification by creating a unique ID and adding it to the panel if it's not already there.
186 |    * @param {Partial<NotificationDTO>} notification - The new notification
187 |    * @param {boolean} isBroadcast - Whether the notification is from a broadcast
188 |    */
189 |   private handleNewNotification(
190 |     notification: Partial<NotificationDTO>,
191 |     isBroadcast: boolean,
192 |   ): void {
193 |     const notificationToSent = {
194 |       id: notification.id,
195 |       message: notification.message!,
196 |       timestamp: notification.timestamp!,
197 |       type: notification.type!,
198 |     };
199 |     const notificationId = `${notification.id}-${notification.timestamp}`;
200 | 
201 |     if (!this.processedNotifications.has(notificationId)) {
202 |       this.processedNotifications.add(notificationId);
203 |       this.addNotification(notificationToSent as NotificationDTO);
204 |       if (isBroadcast) {
205 |         this.incrementUnreadCount();
206 |         this.broadcastChannel.postMessage({ type: 'newNotification', notificationToSent });
207 |       }
208 |       this.showNotification(notificationToSent.message, 'Anzeigen', () =>
209 |         this.handleNotificationClick(notificationToSent as NotificationDTO, 'view').subscribe(),
210 |       );
211 |     }
212 |   }
213 | 
214 |   /**
215 |    * Creates a handler for HTTP errors.
216 |    * @param operation - The name of the operation that failed
217 |    * @param {T} result - The optional result to return as the observable result
218 |    * @returns {Observable<T>} An observable of the result type
219 |    */
220 |   private handleError<T>(operation = 'operation', result?: T): (error: any) => Observable<T> {
221 |     return (error: any): Observable<T> => {
222 |       console.error(`${operation} failed: ${error.message}`);
223 |       // Optionally, we can even send a notification to the user here
224 |       return new Observable<T>(subscriber => {
225 |         subscriber.next(result as T);
226 |         subscriber.complete();
227 |       });
228 |     };
229 |   }
230 | 
231 |   /**
232 |    * Fetches notifications from the server.
233 |    * @param {number} limit - The maximum number of notifications to fetch.
234 |    * @param {number} offset - The offset for pagination.
235 |    * @param {boolean} isInitial - Whether this is the initial fetch or a subsequent one.
236 |    * @returns {Observable<NotificationDTO[]>} An Observable of fetched notifications.
237 |    */
238 |   private fetchNotifications(
239 |     limit: number,
240 |     offset: number,
241 |     isInitial: boolean,
242 |   ): Observable<NotificationDTO[]> {
243 |     return this.http
244 |       .get<NotificationDTO[]>(`${environment.server}/notifications/all`, {
245 |         params: {
246 |           userId: this.userId!.toString(),
247 |           limit: limit.toString(),
248 |           offset: offset.toString(),
249 |         },
250 |       })
251 |       .pipe(
252 |         catchError(this.handleError<NotificationDTO[]>('fetchNotifications', [])),
253 |         map(notifications => {
254 |           if (isInitial) {
255 |             this.notifications = notifications;
256 |           } else {
257 |             this.notifications = [...this.notifications, ...notifications];
258 |           }
259 |           this.notificationsSubject.next(this.notifications);
260 |           if (isInitial) {
261 |             this.syncUnreadCount();
262 |           }
263 |           return notifications;
264 |         }),
265 |       );
266 |   }
267 | 
268 |   /**
269 |    * Fetches the initial notifications from the server.
270 |    * @param {number} limit - The maximum number of notifications to fetch.
271 |    * @param {number} offset - The offset for pagination.
272 |    * @returns {Observable<NotificationDTO[]>} An Observable of fetched notifications.
273 |    */
[TRUNCATED]
```

src/app/Services/progress/progress.service.ts
```
1 | import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
2 | import { GraphCommunicationService } from '../graph/graphCommunication.service';
3 | import { timer, from, Observable } from 'rxjs';
4 | import { concatMap, catchError } from 'rxjs/operators';
5 | import { ContentDTO, ContentElementDTO } from '@DTOs/content.dto';
6 | import { contentElementType } from '@DTOs/contentElementType.enum';
7 | 
8 | @Injectable({
9 |   providedIn: 'root',
10 | })
11 | export class ProgressService {
12 |   /**
13 |    * Instance of GraphCommunicationService (Singleton) for graph-related communications.
14 |    * @private
15 |    */
16 |   private readonly graphCommunicationService: GraphCommunicationService =
17 |     GraphCommunicationService.getInstance();
18 | 
19 |   constructor() {}
20 | 
21 |   /**
22 |    * Triggers an update of the concept graph when an answer is submitted.
23 |    * This method should be called whenever a user completes an action that affects their progress.
24 |    * Uses concatMap to ensure operations happen in sequence and the graph update occurs after backend processing.
25 |    */
26 |   answerSubmitted(): void {
27 |     // Use timer to create a small delay for backend processing
28 |     timer(500)
29 |       .pipe(
30 |         concatMap(() => {
31 |           console.log('Triggering graph update after backend processing (500ms delay)');
32 |           this.graphCommunicationService.triggerGraphUpdate();
33 |           return from(Promise.resolve()); // Convert to Observable for proper chaining
34 |         }),
35 |         catchError(error => {
36 |           console.error('Error updating graph after answer submission:', error);
37 |           return from(Promise.resolve()); // Continue the chain even if there's an error
38 |         }),
39 |       )
40 |       .subscribe();
41 |   }
42 | 
43 |   /**
44 |    * Triggers a graph update when a new question is created
45 |    */
46 |   questionCreated(): void {
47 |     this.graphCommunicationService.triggerGraphUpdate();
48 |   }
49 | 
50 |   /**
51 |    * Triggers a graph update when a question link is deleted
52 |    */
53 |   questionLinkDeleted(): void {
54 |     this.graphCommunicationService.triggerGraphUpdate();
55 |   }
56 | 
57 |   /**
58 |    * Calculates the progress of a given content node by averaging the progress of its question elements.
59 |    *
60 |    * @param {ContentDTO} contentNode - The content node containing content elements.
61 |    * @returns {number} - The average progress of the question elements within the content node.
62 |    */
63 |   calculateProgress(contentNode: ContentDTO): number {
64 |     const questionElements: ContentElementDTO[] = contentNode.contentElements.filter(
65 |       element => element.type === contentElementType.QUESTION,
66 |     );
67 |     const progress =
68 |       questionElements.reduce((acc, element) => {
69 |         return acc + (element.question?.progress || 0);
70 |       }, 0) / questionElements.length;
71 |     return progress;
72 |   }
73 | 
74 |   /**
75 |    * Calculates the highest level of progress for a given content node.
76 |    *
77 |    * This function filters and sorts the content elements of the provided content node
78 |    * to determine the highest level of progress achieved. It considers only elements
79 |    * of type `QUESTION` that have a defined level and progress. The highest level is
80 |    * determined by iterating through the sorted elements and checking their progress.
81 |    *
82 |    * @param contentNode - The content node containing the elements to be evaluated.
83 |    * @returns The highest level of progress achieved for the given content node.
84 |    */
85 |   calculateLevelProgress(contentNode: ContentDTO): number {
86 |     const sortedQuestionElements: ContentElementDTO[] = contentNode.contentElements
87 |       .filter(element => element.type === contentElementType.QUESTION && element.question?.level)
88 |       .sort((a, b) => a.question!.level - b.question!.level);
89 | 
90 |     let highestLevel = 0;
91 |     for (let i = 0; i < sortedQuestionElements.length; i++) {
92 |       const cv = sortedQuestionElements[i];
93 |       if (cv.question!.progress < 100) {
94 |         break;
95 |       } else {
96 |         // only change highestLevel if last element or next element has different (higher) level
97 |         if (
98 |           i == sortedQuestionElements.length - 1 ||
99 |           sortedQuestionElements[i + 1].question!.level != cv.question!.level
100 |         ) {
101 |           highestLevel = cv.question!.level;
102 |         }
103 |       }
104 |     }
105 |     return highestLevel;
106 |   }
107 | }
```

1 | import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
2 | import { Observable } from 'rxjs';
3 | import { of } from 'rxjs';
4 | import {
5 |   FillinQuestionDTO,
6 |   MCOptionViewDTO,
7 |   McQuestionDTO,
8 |   QuestionDTO,
9 |   UserAnswerDataDTO,
10 |   freeTextQuestionDTO,
11 |   userAnswerFeedbackDTO,
12 | } from '@DTOs/index';
13 | import { FillinQuestionType } from '@DTOs/index';
14 | import { MatDialog, MatDialogConfig, MatDialogRef } from '@angular/material/dialog';
15 | import { McTaskComponent } from 'src/app/Pages/contentView/contentElement/mcTask/mcTask.component';
16 | import { FillinTaskNewComponent } from 'src/app/Pages/contentView/contentElement/fill-in-task-new/fill-in-task-new.component';
17 | import { FreeTextTaskComponent } from 'src/app/Pages/contentView/contentElement/free-text-task/free-text-task.component';
18 | 
19 | /**
20 |  * Mock service that provides fake data for question components
21 |  * Used to avoid real HTTP requests when displaying example questions
22 |  */
23 | @Injectable()
24 | export class MockQuestionDataService {
25 |   constructor(private readonly dialog: MatDialog) {}
26 | 
27 |   /**
28 |    * Returns mock question data
29 |    */
30 |   getQuestionData(questionId: number): Observable<QuestionDTO> {
31 |     return of({
32 |       id: questionId,
33 |       name: 'Beispiel Multiple-Choice Frage',
34 |       description: 'Eine Beispielfrage',
35 |       score: 10,
36 |       type: 'MultipleChoice',
37 |       text: 'Welche der folgenden Aussagen sind korrekt?',
38 |       conceptNodeId: 1,
39 |       isApproved: true,
40 |       originId: -1,
41 |       level: 1,
42 |     });
43 |   }
44 | 
45 |   /**
46 |    * Returns mock MC question data
47 |    */
48 |   getMCQuestion(questionVersionId: number): Observable<McQuestionDTO> {
49 |     return of({
50 |       id: questionVersionId,
51 |       questionId: questionVersionId,
52 |       isSC: false,
53 |       shuffleOptions: true,
54 |       questionVersion: {
55 |         id: 1,
56 |         version: 1,
57 |         isApproved: true,
58 |         questionId: questionVersionId,
59 |         successor: null,
60 |       },
61 |       mcQuestionOption: [
62 |         {
63 |           id: 1,
64 |           mcQuestionId: questionVersionId,
65 |           option: { id: 1, text: 'Diese Aussage ist richtig.', correct: true },
66 |         },
67 |         {
68 |           id: 2,
69 |           mcQuestionId: questionVersionId,
70 |           option: { id: 2, text: 'Diese Aussage ist falsch.', correct: false },
71 |         },
72 |         {
73 |           id: 3,
74 |           mcQuestionId: questionVersionId,
75 |           option: { id: 3, text: 'Auch diese Aussage ist richtig.', correct: true },
76 |         },
77 |       ],
78 |     });
79 |   }
80 | 
81 |   /**
82 |    * Returns mock MC options
83 |    */
84 |   getMCOptions(questionId: number): Observable<MCOptionViewDTO[]> {
85 |     return of([
86 |       { id: 1, text: 'Diese Aussage ist richtig.', selected: false },
87 |       { id: 2, text: 'Diese Aussage ist falsch.', selected: false },
88 |       { id: 3, text: 'Auch diese Aussage ist richtig.', selected: false },
89 |     ]);
90 |   }
91 | 
92 |   /**
93 |    * Returns mock free text question data
94 |    */
95 |   getFreeTextQuestion(questionVersionId: number): Observable<freeTextQuestionDTO> {
96 |     return of({
97 |       questionId: questionVersionId,
98 |       contentElementId: 1,
99 |       title: 'Beispiel Freitext Frage',
100 |       text: 'Beschreiben Sie in eigenen Worten, was Sie unter dem Begriff "Programmierung" verstehen.',
101 |       expectations: 'Der Nutzer soll die Grundkonzepte der Programmierung erklären.',
102 |       maxPoints: 10,
103 |     });
104 |   }
105 | 
106 |   /**
107 |    * Returns mock user answer data
108 |    */
109 |   getNewestUserAnswer(questionId: number, userId: number = -1): Observable<UserAnswerDataDTO> {
110 |     return of({
111 |       id: 1,
112 |       questionId: questionId,
113 |       contentElementId: 1,
114 |       userId: userId,
115 |       userFreetextAnswer: 'Dies ist eine Beispielantwort für die Freitext-Aufgabe.',
116 |     });
117 |   }
118 | 
119 |   /**
120 |    * Returns mock fill-in task data
121 |    */
122 |   getFillinTask(questionId: number): Observable<FillinQuestionDTO> {
123 |     return of({
124 |       id: questionId,
125 |       taskType: FillinQuestionType.FillinDrag,
126 |       question: {
127 |         id: questionId,
128 |         name: 'Beispiel Lückentext Aufgabe',
129 |         description: 'Füllen Sie die Lücken aus',
130 |         score: 10,
131 |         type: 'FillIn',
132 |         text: 'Füllen Sie die Lücken im folgenden Text aus.',
133 |         conceptNodeId: 1,
134 |         isApproved: true,
135 |         originId: -1,
136 |         level: 1,
137 |       },
138 |       content: `<p>Angular ist ein <span class="generated-blank" data-position="1"></span> zur Erstellung von <span class="generated-blank" data-position="2"></span>. Es verwendet <span class="generated-blank" data-position="3"></span> als Programmiersprache.</p>`,
139 |       blanks: [
140 |         { id: 1, position: '1', blankContent: 'Framework' },
141 |         { id: 2, position: '2', blankContent: 'Webanwendungen' },
142 |         { id: 3, position: '3', blankContent: 'TypeScript' },
143 |         { id: 4, position: '0', blankContent: 'JavaScript' },
144 |         { id: 5, position: '0', blankContent: 'Komponenten' },
145 |       ],
146 |     });
147 |   }
148 | 
149 |   /**
150 |    * Mock user answer creation that returns success feedback
151 |    */
152 |   createUserAnswer(data: UserAnswerDataDTO): Observable<userAnswerFeedbackDTO> {
153 |     return of({
154 |       id: 1,
155 |       userAnswerId: 1,
156 |       score: 10,
157 |       feedbackText: 'Sehr gut! Alle Antworten sind korrekt.',
158 |       elementDone: true,
159 |       progress: 100,
160 |     });
161 |   }
162 | 
163 |   /**
164 |    * Opens a dialog for a task using the mock service
165 |    */
166 |   openDialog(
167 |     taskType: string,
168 |     config: MatDialogConfig,
169 |   ): MatDialogRef<McTaskComponent | FreeTextTaskComponent | FillinTaskNewComponent> | undefined {
170 |     const modifiedConfig = {
171 |       ...config,
172 |       providers: [{ provide: QuestionDataService, useClass: MockQuestionDataService }],
173 |     };
174 | 
175 |     switch (taskType) {
176 |       case 'SingleChoice':
177 |       case 'MultipleChoice':
178 |         return this.dialog.open(McTaskComponent, modifiedConfig);
179 |       case 'FreeText':
180 |         return this.dialog.open(FreeTextTaskComponent, modifiedConfig);
181 |       case 'FillIn':
182 |         return this.dialog.open(FillinTaskNewComponent, { ...modifiedConfig, width: '50vw' });
183 |       default:
184 |         console.warn(`No dialog defined for task type: ${taskType}`);
185 |         return undefined;
186 |     }
187 |   }
188 | }
189 | 
190 | /**
191 |  * Actual service reference to avoid import error in the mock service
192 |  */
193 | import { QuestionDataService } from './question-data.service';

src/app/Services/question/question-data.service.ts
```
1 | import {
2 |   McQuestionDTO,
3 |   QuestionDTO,
4 |   MCOptionDTO,
5 |   MCOptionViewDTO,
6 |   McQuestionOptionDTO,
7 |   freeTextQuestionDTO,
8 |   detailedQuestionDTO,
9 |   UserAnswerDataDTO,
10 |   userAnswerFeedbackDTO,
11 |   UserMCOptionSelectedDTO,
12 |   FillinQuestionDTO,
13 |   GraphQuestionDTO,
14 |   uploadQuestionDTO,
15 | } from '@DTOs/index';
16 | import { questionType } from '@DTOs/index';
17 | import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http';
18 | import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
19 | import { environment } from 'src/environments/environment';
20 | import { Observable } from 'rxjs';
21 | import { McTaskComponent } from 'src/app/Pages/contentView/contentElement/mcTask/mcTask.component';
22 | import { McSliderTaskComponent } from 'src/app/Pages/contentView/contentElement/mcSliderTask/mc-slider-task.component';
23 | import { FillinTaskNewComponent } from 'src/app/Pages/contentView/contentElement/fill-in-task-new/fill-in-task-new.component';
24 | import { FreeTextTaskComponent } from 'src/app/Pages/contentView/contentElement/free-text-task/free-text-task.component';
25 | import { EditUploadComponent } from 'src/app/Pages/lecturersView/edit-upload/edit-upload.component';
26 | import { MatDialog, MatDialogConfig, MatDialogRef } from '@angular/material/dialog';
27 | 
28 | @Injectable({
29 |   providedIn: 'root',
30 | })
31 | export class QuestionDataService {
32 |   constructor(
33 |     private readonly http: HttpClient,
34 |     private readonly dialog: MatDialog,
35 |   ) {}
36 | 
37 |   /**
38 |    *
39 |    * @param {number} questionId - The ID of the question to retrieve data for
40 |    * @returns {Observable<QuestionDTO>} An Observable that emits the question data
41 |    */
42 |   getQuestionData(questionId: number): Observable<QuestionDTO> {
43 |     return this.http.get<QuestionDTO>(environment.server + `/question-data/${questionId}`);
44 |   }
45 | 
46 |   /**
47 |    * Retrieves detailed question (a question with all its connected specific questions based on the question type) data for a given question ID and type.
48 |    * @param {number} questionId - The ID of the question to retrieve detailed data for.
49 |    * @param {questionType} questionType - The type of the question.
50 |    * @returns {Observable<detailedQuestionDTO>} An Observable that emits a detailedQuestionDTO object.
51 |    */
52 |   getDetailedQuestionData(
53 |     questionId: number,
54 |     questionType: questionType,
55 |   ): Observable<detailedQuestionDTO> {
56 |     return this.http.post<detailedQuestionDTO>(environment.server + `/question-data/detailed`, {
57 |       questionId,
58 |       questionType,
59 |     });
60 |   }
61 | 
62 |   /**
63 |    * Retrieves the newest version of a question.
64 |    * @param {number} questionId - The ID of the question to get the newest version for.
65 |    * @returns {Observable<QuestionDTO>} An Observable that emits the newest QuestionDTO.
66 |    */
67 |   getNewestQuestionVersion(questionId: number): Observable<QuestionDTO> {
68 |     return this.http.get<QuestionDTO>(
69 |       environment.server + `/question-data/newestQuestionVersion/${questionId}`,
70 |     );
71 |   }
72 | 
73 |   /**
74 |    * Gets the most recent answer for a question from a specific user.
75 |    * @param {number} questionId - The ID of the question to get the answer for.
76 |    * @param {number} userId - The ID of the user, defaults to -1 for current user.
77 |    * @returns {Observable<UserAnswerDataDTO>} An Observable that emits the UserAnswerDataDTO.
78 |    */
79 |   getNewestUserAnswer(questionId: number, userId: number = -1): Observable<UserAnswerDataDTO> {
80 |     return this.http.get<UserAnswerDataDTO>(
81 |       environment.server + `/question-data/newestUserAnswer/${questionId}/${userId}`,
82 |     );
83 |   }
84 | 
85 |   /**
86 |    * Retrieves multiple choice question data for a specific question version.
87 |    * @param {number} questionVersionId - The version ID of the question.
88 |    * @returns {Observable<McQuestionDTO>} An Observable that emits the McQuestionDTO.
89 |    */
90 |   getMCQuestion(questionVersionId: number): Observable<McQuestionDTO> {
91 |     return this.http.get<McQuestionDTO>(
92 |       environment.server + `/question-data/mcQuestion/${questionVersionId}`,
93 |     );
94 |   }
95 | 
96 |   /**
97 |    * Gets all multiple choice options for a question.
98 |    * @param {number} questionId - The ID of the question to get options for.
99 |    * @returns {Observable<MCOptionViewDTO[]>} An Observable that emits an array of MCOptionViewDTO.
100 |    */
101 |   getMCOptions(questionId: number): Observable<MCOptionViewDTO[]> {
102 |     return this.http.get<MCOptionViewDTO[]>(
103 |       environment.server + `/question-data/mcOptions/${questionId}`,
104 |     );
105 |   }
106 | 
107 |   /**
108 |    * Retrieves free text question data for a specific question version.
109 |    * @param {number} questionVersionId - The version ID of the question.
110 |    * @returns {Observable<freeTextQuestionDTO>} An Observable that emits the freeTextQuestionDTO.
111 |    */
112 |   getFreeTextQuestion(questionVersionId: number): Observable<freeTextQuestionDTO> {
113 |     return this.http.get<freeTextQuestionDTO>(
114 |       environment.server + `/question-data/freeTextQuestion/${questionVersionId}`,
115 |     );
116 |   }
117 | 
118 |   /**
119 |    * Gets fill-in task data for a specific question.
120 |    * @param {number} questionId - The ID of the fill-in question.
121 |    * @returns {Observable<FillinQuestionDTO>} An Observable that emits the FillinQuestionDTO.
122 |    */
123 |   getFillinTask(questionId: number): Observable<FillinQuestionDTO> {
124 |     return this.http.get<FillinQuestionDTO>(
125 |       environment.server + `/question-data/fillinQuestion/${questionId}`,
126 |     );
127 |   }
128 | 
129 |   /**
130 |    * Retrieves graph question data for a specific question version.
131 |    * @param {number} questionVersionId - The version ID of the question.
132 |    * @returns {Observable<GraphQuestionDTO>} An Observable that emits the GraphQuestionDTO.
133 |    */
134 |   getGraphQuestion(questionVersionId: number): Observable<GraphQuestionDTO> {
135 |     return this.http.get<GraphQuestionDTO>(
136 |       environment.server + `/question-data/graphQuestion/${questionVersionId}`,
137 |     );
138 |   }
139 | 
140 |   /**
141 |    * Retrieves upload question data for a specific question version.
142 |    * @param {number} questionVersionId - The version ID of the question.
143 |    * @returns {Observable<uploadQuestionDTO>} An Observable that emits the uploadQuestionDTO.
144 |    */
145 |   getUploadQuestion(questionVersionId: number): Observable<uploadQuestionDTO> {
146 |     return this.http.get<uploadQuestionDTO>(
147 |       environment.server + `/question-data/uploadQuestion/${questionVersionId}`,
148 |     );
149 |   }
150 | 
151 |   /**
152 |    * Creates a new user answer for a question.
153 |    * @param {UserAnswerDataDTO} data - The user answer data containing question response information.
154 |    * @returns {Observable<userAnswerFeedbackDTO>} An Observable that emits the feedback for the submitted answer.
155 |    */
156 |   createUserAnswer(data: UserAnswerDataDTO): Observable<userAnswerFeedbackDTO> {
157 |     return this.http.post<userAnswerFeedbackDTO>(
158 |       environment.server + `/question-data/userAnswer/create`,
159 |       data,
160 |     );
161 |   }
162 | 
163 |   /**
164 |    * Gets the progress of a question for the currently authenticated user.
165 |    * @param {number} questionId - The ID of the question.
166 |    * @returns {Observable<{ progress: number }>} An Observable with the progress in percent.
167 |    */
168 |   getQuestionProgress(questionId: number): Observable<{ progress: number }> {
169 |     return this.http.get<{ progress: number }>(
170 |       environment.server + `/question-data/progress/${questionId}`,
171 |     );
172 |   }
173 | 
174 |   /**
175 |    * Fetches the contentNodeIds and contentElementIds associated with a specific question.
176 |    *
177 |    * @param {number} questionId - The ID of the question.
178 |    * @returns {Observable<{contentNodeId: number, contentElementId: number}>} An Observable that emits a ContentIdsDTO object.
179 |    */
180 |   getContentIds(
181 |     questionId: number,
182 |   ): Observable<{ contentNodeId: number; contentElementId: number }> {
183 |     return this.http.get<{ contentNodeId: number; contentElementId: number }>(
184 |       environment.server + `/question-data/contentIds/${questionId}`,
185 |     );
186 |   }
187 | 
188 |   /**
189 |    * Creates a new user MC option selection record.
190 |    * Links a user's answer to a specific multiple choice option.
191 |    *
192 |    * @param {number} userAnswerId - The ID of the user's answer
193 |    * @param {number} mcOptionId - The ID of the selected multiple choice option
194 |    * @returns {Observable<UserMCOptionSelectedDTO>} An Observable that emits the created UserMCOptionSelectedDTO
195 |    */
196 |   createUserMCOptionSelected(
197 |     userAnswerId: number,
198 |     mcOptionId: number,
199 |   ): Observable<UserMCOptionSelectedDTO> {
200 |     return this.http.post<UserMCOptionSelectedDTO>(
201 |       environment.server + `/question-data/userMCOptionSelected/create`,
202 |       { userAnswerId, mcOptionId },
203 |     );
204 |   }
205 | 
206 |   /**
207 |    * Creates a new question in the system.
208 |    *
209 |    * @param {QuestionDTO} question - The question data to create
210 |    * @returns {Observable<QuestionDTO>} An Observable that emits the created QuestionDTO
211 |    */
212 |   createQuestion(question: QuestionDTO): Observable<QuestionDTO> {
213 |     return this.http.post<QuestionDTO>(
214 |       environment.server + `/question-data/createQuestion`,
215 |       question,
216 |     );
217 |   }
218 | 
219 |   /**
220 |    * Updates an existing question's basic information.
221 |    *
222 |    * @param {QuestionDTO} question - The question data to update
223 |    * @returns {Observable<QuestionDTO>} An Observable that emits the updated QuestionDTO
224 |    */
225 |   updateQuestion(question: QuestionDTO): Observable<QuestionDTO> {
226 |     return this.http.put<QuestionDTO>(
227 |       environment.server + `/question-data/updateQuestion`,
228 |       question,
229 |     );
230 |   }
231 | 
232 |   /**
233 |    * Updates all aspects of a question including associated data.
234 |    * This method updates the complete question structure including any nested components.
235 |    *
236 |    * @param {detailedQuestionDTO} question - The detailed question data containing all components to update
237 |    * @returns {Observable<detailedQuestionDTO>} An Observable that emits the updated detailedQuestionDTO
238 |    */
239 |   updateWholeQuestion(question: detailedQuestionDTO): Observable<detailedQuestionDTO> {
240 |     return this.http.post<detailedQuestionDTO>(
241 |       environment.server + `/question-data/updateWholeQuestion`,
242 |       question,
243 |     );
244 |   }
245 | 
246 |   /**
247 |    * Creates a new version of an existing question with updates.
248 |    * Instead of modifying the existing question, this creates a new version with the changes.
249 |    *
250 |    * @param {detailedQuestionDTO} question - The detailed question data for the new version
251 |    * @returns {Observable<detailedQuestionDTO>} An Observable that emits the newly created question version as detailedQuestionDTO
252 |    */
[TRUNCATED]
```

src/app/Services/websocket/websocket.service.ts
```
1 | import { OnDestroy } from '@angular/core';
2 | import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
3 | import { Socket, ManagerOptions, SocketOptions } from 'socket.io-client';
4 | import { io } from 'socket.io-client';
5 | import { Observable, Subject, BehaviorSubject, timer } from 'rxjs';
6 | import { UserService } from '../auth/user.service';
7 | import { takeUntil, retry, switchMap } from 'rxjs/operators';
8 | 
9 | interface SocketConnection {
10 |   socket: Socket;
11 |   connectionSubject: BehaviorSubject<boolean>;
12 | }
13 | /**
14 |  * Service for managing WebSocket connections.
15 |  */
16 | @Injectable({
17 |   providedIn: 'root',
18 | })
19 | export class WebSocketService implements OnDestroy {
20 |   /** A map of WebSocket connections, indexed by the full URL of the connection.
21 |    * The value is an object containing the socket instance and a BehaviorSubject for the connection status.*/
22 |   private readonly sockets: Map<string, SocketConnection> = new Map();
23 |   private readonly destroyed$ = new Subject<void>();
24 | 
25 |   constructor(private readonly userService: UserService) {}
26 | 
27 |   /**
28 |    * Initiates a WebSocket connection.
29 |    * @param {string} url - The base URL for the WebSocket connection.
30 |    * @param {string} namespace - The namespace for the WebSocket connection.
31 |    * @param {ManagerOptions & SocketOptions} options - Additional options for the connection.
32 |    */
33 |   connect(
34 |     url: string,
35 |     namespace: string,
36 |     options: Partial<ManagerOptions & SocketOptions> = {},
37 |   ): void {
38 |     const fullUrl = `${url}/${namespace}`;
39 |     if (!this.sockets.has(fullUrl)) {
40 |       const socket = io(fullUrl, {
41 |         query: { token: this.userService.getAccessToken() },
42 |         withCredentials: true, // Required for cross-origin requests
43 |         transports: ['websocket'],
44 |         ...options, // example usage of options: { autoConnect: false, reconnectionAttempts: 5, timeout: 10000, .. }
45 |       });
46 | 
47 |       const connectionSubject = new BehaviorSubject<boolean>(false);
48 | 
49 |       socket.on('connect', () => {
50 |         console.log(`WebSocket connected: ${fullUrl}`);
51 |         connectionSubject.next(true);
52 |       });
53 | 
54 |       socket.on('disconnect', () => {
55 |         console.log(`WebSocket disconnected: ${fullUrl}`);
56 |         connectionSubject.next(false);
57 |       });
58 | 
59 |       socket.on('connect_error', (error: Error) => {
60 |         console.error(`Connection error for ${fullUrl}:`, error);
61 |         this.handleReconnection(fullUrl);
62 |       });
63 | 
64 |       this.sockets.set(fullUrl, { socket, connectionSubject });
65 |     }
66 |   }
67 | 
68 |   /**
69 |    * Disconnects the WebSocket.
70 |    * @param {string} url - The base URL of the WebSocket connection.
71 |    * @param {string} namespace - The namespace of the WebSocket connection.
72 |    */
73 |   disconnect(url: string, namespace: string): void {
74 |     const fullUrl = `${url}/${namespace}`;
75 |     const connection = this.sockets.get(fullUrl);
76 |     if (connection) {
77 |       connection.socket.disconnect();
78 |       connection.connectionSubject.complete();
79 |       this.sockets.delete(fullUrl);
80 |     }
81 |   }
82 | 
83 |   /**
84 |    * Listens for a specific event on the WebSocket.
85 |    * @param {string} url - The base URL of the WebSocket connection.
86 |    * @param {string} namespace - The namespace of the WebSocket connection.
87 |    * @param {string} eventName - The name of the event to listen for.
88 |    * @returns {Observable<T>} An Observable that emits the data received for the event.
89 |    */
90 |   on<T>(url: string, namespace: string, eventName: string): Observable<T> {
91 |     return new Observable<T>(observer => {
92 |       const socket = this.getSocket(url, namespace);
93 |       if (!socket) {
94 |         observer.error(new Error(`No socket connection for ${url}/${namespace}`));
95 |         return;
96 |       }
97 | 
98 |       const handler = (data: T) => observer.next(data);
99 |       socket.on(eventName, handler);
100 | 
101 |       return () => {
102 |         socket.off(eventName, handler);
103 |       };
104 |     });
105 |   }
106 | 
107 |   /**
108 |    * Emits an event with data to the WebSocket server.
109 |    * @param {string} url - The base URL of the WebSocket connection.
110 |    * @param {string} namespace - The namespace of the WebSocket connection.
111 |    * @param {string} eventName - The name of the event to emit.
112 |    * @param {any} data - The data to send with the event.
113 |    */
114 |   emit(url: string, namespace: string, eventName: string, data: any): void {
115 |     const socket = this.getSocket(url, namespace);
116 |     if (socket) {
117 |       socket.emit(eventName, data);
118 |     } else {
119 |       console.error(`No socket connection for ${url}/${namespace}`);
120 |     }
121 |   }
122 | 
123 |   /**
124 |    * Returns an Observable for the connection status.
125 |    * @param {string} url - The base URL of the WebSocket connection.
126 |    * @param {string} namespace - The namespace of the WebSocket connection.
127 |    * @returns {Observable<boolean>} An Observable that emits true when connected and false when disconnected.
128 |    */
129 |   onConnectionChange(url: string, namespace: string): Observable<boolean> {
130 |     const connection = this.sockets.get(`${url}/${namespace}`);
131 |     return connection ? connection.connectionSubject.asObservable() : new Observable<boolean>();
132 |   }
133 | 
134 |   /**
135 |    * Checks if a specific WebSocket connection is currently connected.
136 |    * @param {string} url - The base URL of the WebSocket connection.
137 |    * @param {string} namespace - The namespace of the WebSocket connection.
138 |    * @returns {boolean} The connection status.
139 |    */
140 |   isConnected(url: string, namespace: string): boolean {
141 |     const socket = this.getSocket(url, namespace);
142 |     return socket?.connected || false;
143 |   }
144 | 
145 |   /**
146 |    * Handles reconnection attempts for a disconnected socket.
147 |    * @param {string} fullUrl - The full URL of the socket to reconnect.
148 |    */
149 |   private handleReconnection(fullUrl: string): void {
150 |     timer(0, 5000)
151 |       .pipe(
152 |         takeUntil(this.destroyed$),
153 |         switchMap(() => {
154 |           const connection = this.sockets.get(fullUrl);
155 |           if (connection && !connection.socket.connected) {
156 |             console.log(`Attempting to reconnect to ${fullUrl}...`);
157 |             connection.socket.connect();
158 |             return connection.connectionSubject;
159 |           }
160 |           return new Observable<boolean>();
161 |         }),
162 |         retry(),
163 |         takeUntil(timer(60000)), // Stop trying after 1 minute
164 |       )
165 |       .subscribe();
166 |   }
167 | 
168 |   /**
169 |    * Retrieves a socket instance for the given URL and namespace.
170 |    * @param {string} url - The base URL of the WebSocket connection.
171 |    * @param {string} namespace - The namespace of the WebSocket connection.
172 |    * @returns {Socket | undefined} The socket instance if it exists, undefined otherwise.
173 |    */
174 |   private getSocket(url: string, namespace: string): Socket | undefined {
175 |     return this.sockets.get(`${url}/${namespace}`)?.socket;
176 |   }
177 | 
178 |   /**
179 |    * Unsubscribes from all Observables and disconnects all WebSocket connections.
180 |    */
181 |   ngOnDestroy(): void {
182 |     this.destroyed$.next();
183 |     this.destroyed$.complete();
184 |     this.sockets.forEach(connection => {
185 |       connection.socket.disconnect();
186 |       connection.connectionSubject.complete();
187 |     });
188 |     this.sockets.clear();
189 |   }
190 | }
```
